# Link and source quality instructions

Rules for evaluating links and sources before adding or editing an entry.

- Prefer papers, official repositories, official documentation, institutional pages, and project pages.
- Use the canonical home of a resource, not secondary coverage or mirrors.
- Use HTTPS links.
- Avoid duplicate links across sections.
- Avoid dead links. Confirm the URL resolves before adding it.
- Avoid low-quality promotional sources and thin landing pages.
- Where relevant, check that the resource is still maintained (recent commits, releases, or updates).
- For papers, prefer a stable identifier (arXiv abstract page or DOI) over a hosted PDF.
- For models, prefer the official repository or model card over third-party reuploads.
